markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' confirmed November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI, following two official delays from the original 2025 window, anchors trader sentiment in this multi-outcome market. Recent Take-Two Interactive earnings commentary and executive statements have reinforced that the title remains on track for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, shifting focus to summer marketing milestones such as additional trailers and promotional pushes expected in the coming months. Industry observers note Rockstar's emphasis on polish over rushed timelines, which has historically extended campaigns and created windows for unrelated events like music releases or geopolitical developments to resolve beforehand. With the launch still roughly six months away, upcoming earnings calls and any new gameplay reveals will likely influence which outcomes gain or lose momentum among traders assessing timing risks.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial has sharply reinforced trader consensus around “No Prison Time” at 75.9%. A deadlocked jury on May 15 ended deliberations without a verdict on the core allegations, stalling momentum for additional convictions that could add meaningful time. The disgraced Hollywood producer already faces an appealed 16-year California sentence and potential sentencing on a separate New York count carrying up to 25 years, yet appeals processes, time served, and documented health concerns tilt sentiment toward limited or no incremental incarceration. Upcoming decisions on retrial or sentencing will serve as the next key catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final matchup between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights shapes current trader consensus, with the Hurricanes holding a modest edge after posting the Eastern Conference's best regular-season mark at 53-22-7. Carolina advanced by rallying from an early deficit to defeat the Montreal Canadiens in the conference finals, while Vegas earned a decisive sweep of the Colorado Avalanche to reach its first final since 2023. These recent playoff results, combined with Carolina's home-ice advantage and overall depth, underpin the implied probabilities reflected in the market. Montreal's series loss accounts for its minimal positioning.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: European powerhouses lead the field for the 2026 FIFA World Cup due to strong qualification campaigns and recent tournament pedigree, with Spain and France trading narrow edges as the top contenders amid deep attacking options and defensive stability. England sits close behind thanks to consistent results and squad depth, while Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina maintain realistic paths through individual talent and historical pedigree. The bunched probabilities reflect a wide-open race among several sides with comparable recent form, head-to-head records, and favorable draw positioning, where any injury setbacks or momentum shifts in the group stage could quickly reorder the implied probabilities among the leading nations.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s consolidated authority as General Secretary and Central Military Commission chairman underpins the 92.8% trader consensus against his removal before the end of 2026. Recent military leadership purges have eliminated potential rivals and reinforced centralized control, while the Politburo Standing Committee remains staffed almost entirely by Xi-aligned officials with no visible successor or factional challenge emerging. Ongoing lower-level personnel adjustments ahead of the 21st Party Congress in late 2027 signal continuity rather than transition, and Xi’s public diplomatic schedule and policy addresses have projected stability. Only an unforeseen health event or sudden elite revolt could alter this positioning before the market’s December 31, 2026 resolution.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Democratic presidential nomination for 2028 remains highly fluid in an open field without an incumbent, as reflected in the dispersed trader consensus. Gavin Newsom leads with the highest implied probability owing to his national visibility and record as California governor, followed by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris, whose positioning draws from distinct ideological and prior campaign bases. Josh Shapiro, Pete Buttigieg, and other listed contenders trail with support tied to state-level records, Senate experience, or media profiles. Key differentiators include fundraising capacity, primary-state appeal, and coalition-building potential among swing-state voters or party factions. Support could consolidate through early polling trends, endorsements from elected officials, or standout performances in debates and caucuses ahead of the 2028 cycle.
